title: cynanchica tephrocarpa subsp. exasperata
intro: Cynanchica tephrocarpa subsp. exasperata is a subspecies of the Cynanchica tephrocarpa plant, commonly known as the "Tephrocarpa" or "Exasperating Milkweed", a perennial vine in the Apocynaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Cynanchica tephrocarpa subsp. exasperata is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and its roots and leaves are used to make herbal remedies.
